data.narrative:
Creotech Instruments is a publicly traded Space & Aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Brona, having raised €52M+ in contracts. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

Peer-median methodology is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across these four public Space & Aerospace peers stands at $6.7B, with a range spanning $900M at the low end (Astroscale) to $37.0B at the high end (Rocket Lab). This method directly anchors on companies with aligned sector exposure and public market transparency. A secondary P/S approach using the broader public Space & Aerospace sector median of 59.8x across 6 tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.7B to $7.6B, centered on a $6.7B median. This range reflects the substantial valuation dispersion within the peer set, particularly the 41x spread between Astroscale and Rocket Lab, which signals material variance in company-specific execution, market position, and growth trajectory. Key uncertainties include Creotech's revenue scale relative to peers and the degree to which its contract base ($52M+) translates to profitable recurring revenue—metrics unavailable in the current data set.
